articles.title: Oil prices jump back over $US100 a barrel as Middle East conflict widens
articles.description: The entry of the Houthi rebels into the war between the US and Iran threatens another vital shipping route through the region, as the entire region remains on high alert.

articles.title: Origin breach could fuel wave of AI-powered scams, cyber experts warn
articles.description: The theft of names, addresses and phone numbers may seem less serious than stolen bank details, but cyber experts say AI is changing what criminals can do with that information.

articles.title: Labor warned regional funding woes will drive One Nation support
articles.description: The ALP's 50th National Conference kicked off with the rise of One Nation, local council budgets and regional voter frustrations leading negotiations.

articles.title: Queensland's 2011 floods inspire underground dance culture
articles.description: Equal parts captivating and unsettling, young people from Ipswich to Japan are taking to the streets to dance "flood" style.

articles.title: Probe into claims platypus burrow destroyed by council contractors
articles.description: An investigation is launched after wildlife advocates say a platypus burrow was destroyed by an excavator being used for flood mitigation works in northern Tasmania.

articles.title: How a series of urgent texts warned of a major corruption scandal
articles.description: Nineteen months before making the parliamentary speech that sparked a corruption inquiry into NSW Liberal figures and their relationship with a fugitive property developer, parliamentary secretary Ray Williams sent an urgent text message to his boss, premier Gladys Berejiklian.

articles.title: Live: Parade of athletes begins after King Charles arrives by TARDIS
articles.description: The Games will officially be opened by King Charles as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators.

related.title: Commonwealth Games LIVE: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive in Dr Who Tardis for Glasgow opening ceremony
related.description: The 2026 Commonwealth Games begin with the Opening Ceremony in Glasgow, with Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge leading Australia out as the flag and baton bearers. Follow all the action.
related.normalizedTitle: commonwealth games live king charles and queen camilla arrive in dr who tardis for glasgow opening ceremony

articles.title: Interest rates don't just hit households. Small business is feeling the pinch
articles.description: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

articles.title: How China won the car wars
articles.description: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

articles.title: Adolescence creator swaps therapy for chains in new movie
articles.description: Emmy Award-winning Adolescence co-creator Stephen Graham tackles teen delinquency in a very different way in The Good Boy.

articles.title: The US' $166 billion refund comes with a much bigger cost
articles.description: Trump's willingness to use tariffs to create persistent uncertainty signals a greater economic and legal danger.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
articles.description: Donald Trump is insisting the US deal allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a nuclear energy industry has one major condition — the Arab kingdom must formally recognise the State of Israel.

articles.title: Violence by a son-in-law left Maria 'homeless and hopeless' at 72
articles.description: Reports of elder abuse in Queensland have steadily increased in recent years, with women being the most common victims. Advocates say urgent investment is needed for prevention and response.

articles.title: Fast food key to farmers making more with their dairy calves
articles.description: Dairy beef has caught the attention of fast food companies as farmers beef up bobby calves into viable assets.

articles.title: This footy club wants better for its women's team but is in a pinch
articles.description: Macedon Football Netball Club is proud to do things its way. But it is in a pinch with its women and girls team.

articles.title: Chinese research ships that visit Australia under US surveillance
articles.description: Two Chinese polar research ships that make regular port visits in Australia have come under increased scrutiny by US authorities targeting "any detected malign activity".

articles.title: First nursery for babies of Ebola victims opens in Congo
articles.description: The first UNICEF-supported Ebola nursery opens in the Democratic Republic of Congo, supporting young children whose parents have contracted the disease while the number of cases intensifies.

articles.title: 'Far more' needs to be done to help children leaving state care
articles.description: In Queensland, the Family and Children's Commissioner Luke Twyford, said the situation is "profoundly upsetting".

articles.title: No, AusAlert isn't tracking you (and other conspiracy theories)
articles.description: From wrapping your phone in foil to secret phones, ABC NEWS Verify takes a look into some of these false claims and real concerns about AusAlert to try to answer a few questions.

articles.title: Queensland-style inquiry needed for Victoria's Big Build, says academic
articles.description: One of Australia's leading public policy academics says a "Queensland-style" commission of inquiry is needed into Victoria's Big Build.

articles.title: Gippsland CFA volunteers seek compensation as cancer cluster cases grow
articles.description: In Nar Nar Goon's fire brigade, 22 volunteers have been diagnosed with cancer. But as members work to obtain their work insurance cover, almost half of the members are not covered by the state's presumptive legislation.

articles.title: Charli xcx follows Brat with her most reflective album yet
articles.description: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.

articles.title: Friday news quiz: World Cup winners, rock legends and Barnaby Joyce's dog
articles.description: We have 10 questions to test your knowledge of the week in … SIT DOWN! Sorry, it's the ABC news quiz.

articles.title: Qantas plane set to arrive in Australia today - direct from France
articles.description: The custom-built Airbus A350-1000ULR is expected to land in Melbourne on Friday after making a direct 20-hour plus flight from the factory in Toulouse.

articles.title: Tens of thousands forced to flee French holiday spot as fires spread across Europe
articles.description: Flames are moving rapidly through the bayside area in southern France busy with families on school holidays as tinderbox conditions persist in Europe.

articles.title: As temperatures flip, Australia's ski resorts rely on overnight 'saviour'
articles.description: As winters become less predictable, Australia's ski resorts are turning to manufactured snow to keep the slopes open.

articles.title: Could I endure a frigid Canberra winter? Not in my wildest, electric dreams | Brigid Delaney
articles.description: The best part of my day was sliding into that boiling bed! But then I’d wake at 1am like a piece of lasagne that is burnt on one side and frozen on the other One of my earliest memories as a child is my little hands plugging in the old electric blanket for my visiting grandmother and then being violently thrown across the room after it short-circuited. articles.title: Pauline Hanson blames ‘fake news’ media for her bad press. But serious leaders have to own their actions

articles.title: ‘Says more about the crowd than it does about Isaac’: Pendlebury speaks out on Crows fans booing Quaynor
articles.description: The VFL/AFL games record holder doesn’t believe Isaac Quaynor was rattled by the Adelaide Oval reception, but said he and his fellow Magpies will rally around the gun backman regardless.
